title: Crispy Chicken Bites
cScaleSummary: C2 because the cholesterol comes from the boneless skinless chicken thighs, the eggs in the wash, and the grated Parmesan in the coating. The cornmeal, cornstarch, spices, and frying oil do not contribute cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because the dish is centered on animal protein, egg, cheese, and frying rather than a fiber-forward structure built around legumes, whole grains, nuts, seeds, or vegetables that actively support LDL lowering.
intro: Chicken cutlets are usually a sit-down thing. This version turns them into crisp, savory bites you can spear with a pick and eat in one or two bites.

items.prep: divided (pulse 80% to a finer texture, leave 20% coarse)

items.item: Neutral oil (avocado or similar), enough for shallow frying

stepGroups.title: Prep the Chicken
items: Trim excess fat from the thighs. Cut each thigh into 8–10 bite-size pieces.
Season with salt and pepper and let sit 15–20 minutes.

stepGroups.title: Prepare the Cornmeal
items: Pulse about 80% of the cornmeal in a blender until it reaches a fine sand texture. Leave the remaining 20% as-is. Combine them back together.

stepGroups.title: Build the Coating
items: Mix the cornmeal blend with parmesan, cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika.

stepGroups.title: Egg Wash
items: Beat the eggs with water until smooth.

stepGroups.title: Coat and Set
items: Dip chicken pieces into egg, then into the coating. Press lightly so it adheres but stays thin.
Lay the coated pieces on a sheet pan in a single layer and freeze uncovered for 45–60 minutes, until firm but not rock solid.

stepGroups.title: Fry
items: Heat about ¼ inch of oil over medium-high heat until shimmering. Fry in batches without crowding.
Cook 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden and cooked through, allowing slightly more time due to the chilled chicken.
Transfer immediately to a wire rack.

stepGroups.title: Post-Fry Dust
items: Within 30–60 seconds of coming out of the oil, while still hot and lightly oily, sprinkle or lightly toss with the post-fry dust. Use a very light hand.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Pile the chicken onto a platter and serve warm or at room temperature as one- or two-bite pieces.
